name: VibecodeApp control-layer PRD
description: Detailed implementation PRD for the next VibecodeApp control-layer work. The repository already has a CLI-first architecture-map and context-pack core. The next work must harden relevance scoring, add strict agent-facing AGENTS.md/export workflow, implement guard/check/handoff enforcement, add durable history summaries, add a safe OpenCode adapter, and improve CLI UX with a project registry. Do not implement GUI, MCP, swarm, or broad memory systems in this PRD. Generated/runtime artifacts must remain ignored and must not become source of truth.

tasks.title: Harden relevant-file scoring with two-pass source/test pairing
tasks.description:
Purpose: reduce long-tail noise from unrelated tests by making test boosts depend on direct source relevance.

Current issue:
Many unrelated test files can receive pairing/extension scores and appear in the long tail. Top results are now mostly correct, but the context pack still gets noisy.

Scope:
- Work mainly in `vibecode/context/scoring.py`.
- Update or add tests in `tests/test_vibecode_relevant_files.py`.
- Do not change repo tree rendering.
- Do not implement AGENTS.md, guard, check runner, or OpenCode integration in this task.

Required behavior:
1. First pass: identify directly relevant source files using task/domain signals, exact token/path matches, compound phrase routing, architecture/handoff/history signals, and dependency hints.
2. Second pass: boost test files only when they are paired with directly relevant source files.
3. Do not boost every test just because it has any source pairing.
4. Keep good pairings such as `scoring.py` to `test_vibecode_relevant_files.py`, `repo_tree.py` to `test_vibecode_repo_tree.py`, and `renderer.py` to `test_vibecode_context_pack.py`.

Acceptance:
- For task `Improve relevant-file scoring`, top results include `vibecode/context/scoring.py` and `tests/test_vibecode_relevant_files.py`.
- Unrelated tests do not flood top 10.
- Repo tree and context pack task scenarios still rank their implementation/test files correctly.
- Tests cover the two-pass behavior.

tasks.title: Add compound phrase routing for task domains
tasks.description:
Purpose: make scoring understand domain phrases instead of over-weighting broad single tokens like `context`.

Current issue:
For `Improve context pack rendering`, files under `vibecode/context/` may rank because of the broad token `context`, even if they are not the renderer.

Scope:
- Work in `vibecode/context/scoring.py`.
- Tests in `tests/test_vibecode_relevant_files.py`.
- No LLM/API calls.

Required phrase/domain routes:
- `context pack`, `context rendering`, `context pack rendering` → `vibecode/context/renderer.py`, `tests/test_vibecode_context_pack.py`, `vibecode/context/scoring.py`
- `repo tree`, `architecture tree`, `architecture map rendering` → `vibecode/indexer/repo_tree.py`, `tests/test_vibecode_repo_tree.py`
- `opencode prompt`, `platform export`, `prompt export` → `vibecode/context/platform_export.py`, `vibecode/context/platform_registry.py`, `tests/test_vibecode_platform_export.py`
- `agents export`, `AGENTS.md` → `vibecode/context/agents_export.py`, `tests/test_vibecode_agents_export.py`
- `required checks`, `check runner` → `.vibecode/checks/required_checks.yaml`, future check runner files once implemented
- `guard`, `protected paths` → future guard/protected paths files once implemented

Acceptance:
- Phrase matches are deterministic and documented in code comments or constants.
- Broad token `context` alone is weak; compound phrase `context pack rendering` is strong.
- `platform_registry.py` does not outrank `renderer.py` for context-pack rendering tasks.
- OpenCode/platform tasks still rank platform files high.

tasks.title: Refine dependency boost to avoid hub fan-out
tasks.description:
Purpose: prevent generic hub files such as `cli.py` from spreading relevance to unrelated files.

Scope:
- Work in `vibecode/context/scoring.py` and tests.
- Do not change dependency map generation unless a small schema read fix is required.

Rules:
1. Define deterministic hub/gateway files such as `vibecode/cli.py`, `vibecode/__init__.py`, `vibecode/context/__init__.py`, `vibecode/indexer/__init__.py`, and optionally `vibecode/config.py` / `vibecode/project.py`.
2. Do not apply dependency boost through hub files unless the task explicitly mentions CLI/command/init/config/project behavior.
3. A dependency-connected target must also have direct task/domain relevance or be paired with a directly relevant source/test file.
4. Cap fan-out from any single source file.

Acceptance:
- For `Improve relevant-file scoring`, `vibecode/cli.py` is not top 3 and ideally not in scored results.
- For `Improve context command help`, `vibecode/cli.py` ranks high.
- Dependency reasons remain visible only where meaningful.
- Tests cover both scoring task and CLI task.

tasks.title: Add relevant-file scoring quality smoke tests
tasks.description:
Purpose: lock in expected scoring quality across representative tasks so future changes do not silently degrade context packs.

Scope:
- Add tests in `tests/test_vibecode_relevant_files.py`.
- Source changes are allowed only if tests reveal real bugs.

Test scenarios:
1. `Improve relevant-file scoring` → scoring implementation and its tests, no generated/runtime top results.
2. `Improve repo tree rendering` → repo tree generator and tests.
3. `Improve context pack rendering` → renderer and context-pack tests; platform registry does not outrank renderer.
4. `Add OpenCode prompt export behavior` → platform export, platform registry, and platform export tests.
5. `Add root AGENTS.md` → agents export and AGENTS tests.
6. `Implement guard command` → guard/protected path files once created; until then docs/check policy files can rank as supporting files.

Acceptance:
- Tests use top-N contains/excludes, not brittle full-order assertions unless intentional.
- Tests document behavior expected by future agents.

tasks.title: Add root AGENTS.md with strict agent instructions
tasks.description:
Purpose: provide future coding agents a short root-level instruction file that points them to Vibecode project truth and context workflow.

Scope:
- Create root `AGENTS.md`.
- Keep it short and concrete.
- Do not duplicate README.
- Do not include roadmap, broad philosophy, or long architecture explanations.

Content requirements:
AGENTS.md must instruct agents to read `.vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md`, `.vibecode/architecture/STRUCTURE.md`, and `.vibecode/handoff/NOW.md` when present. It must tell agents to generate or inspect a task-specific context pack with `python -m vibecode.cli context . --task "<task>"`, start from `Relevant files with reasons`, treat source/tests/human-maintained architecture docs as truth, and treat `.vibecode/current/*`, `.vibecode/index/*.generated.*`, `.vibecode/generated/*`, `.vibecode/logs/*` as generated/runtime.

Acceptance:
- Root `AGENTS.md` exists.
- It is short enough to be read quickly by agents.
- It does not tell agents to trust stale generated artifacts.
- Existing tests pass.

tasks.title: Harden AGENTS export workflow safety
tasks.description:
Purpose: make `vibecode export-agents .` safe and predictable.

Existing behavior:
AGENTS export support exists, but the workflow must be explicit and tested.

Scope:
- Work in `vibecode/context/agents_export.py`, CLI dispatch, and tests.
- Update docs only if behavior changes.
- Do not implement OpenCode run integration.

Required behavior:
1. Generated content is written to `.vibecode/generated/AGENTS.generated.md`.
2. Root `AGENTS.md` is either Vibecode-managed and safe to update, or manual and not overwritten unless `--force` is explicitly passed.
3. If root `AGENTS.md` exists and is manual, command exits with clear message and non-zero or warning status according to existing CLI conventions.
4. `--force` must be explicit and tested.
5. Generated files remain ignored/untracked.

Acceptance:
- `export-agents` never silently overwrites a manual root `AGENTS.md`.
- Tests cover no existing AGENTS, managed AGENTS, manual AGENTS without force, and manual AGENTS with force.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_vibecode_agents_export.py -p no:cacheprovider` passes.

tasks.title: Include AGENTS export workflow in context and docs
tasks.description:
Purpose: make agent-facing docs explain how AGENTS.md relates to generated context packs.

Scope:
- Update `README.md`, `docs/QUICKSTART.md`, or relevant docs only.
- Do not add large roadmap text.
- Do not modify implementation unless docs reveal a real mismatch.

Documentation must state:
- Root `AGENTS.md` is stable agent instruction.
- `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md` is task-specific runtime output.
- `.vibecode/generated/AGENTS.generated.md` is generated and ignored.
- `export-agents` must not overwrite manual root AGENTS without `--force`.
- Future agents should generate context for each task rather than relying on old context packs.

Acceptance:
- Docs are concise and consistent with code.
- No version labels are introduced.
- Tests still pass.

tasks.title: Create protected paths policy file and schema
tasks.description:
Purpose: define what files/folders are protected before implementing guard enforcement.

Scope:
- Add `.vibecode/checks/protected_paths.yaml`.
- Add loader/schema code if needed.
- Add tests for parsing and default content.
- Do not implement full `guard` command in this task unless minimal loading CLI help is required.

Default policy should include `.vibecode/architecture/`, `.vibecode/checks/`, `.vibecode/handoff/`, `.vibecode/index/*.generated.*`, `.vibecode/current/*`, `vibecode/indexer/scanner.py`, `vibecode/indexer/repo_tree.py`, `vibecode/context/scoring.py`, and `vibecode/context/renderer.py`, each with a concrete rule and required tests where relevant.

Acceptance:
- Policy file is committed.
- Loader validates required fields.
- Invalid entries produce clear errors.
- Context pack can later include protected path summary.

tasks.title: Expose protected paths in context pack
tasks.description:
Purpose: make agents see protected paths before editing.

Prerequisite:
Protected paths policy file exists and can be loaded.

Scope:
- Update context rendering to include protected paths from `.vibecode/checks/protected_paths.yaml`.
- Do not implement guard enforcement yet.
- Keep output compact.

Required output:
Context pack includes a section like `## Protected paths / edit constraints` with path, reason/rule, required tests if defined, and whether explicit task scope is required.

Acceptance:
- Protected paths appear in context pack.
- Generated/runtime paths are clearly labeled as not manually editable.
- Tests cover rendering.

tasks.title: Implement git diff collection utility
tasks.description:
Purpose: create a reusable internal utility for guard/check/run workflows to inspect changed files.

Scope:
- Add a small module, for example `vibecode/git_state.py` or `vibecode/guard/git_diff.py`.
- Do not implement full guard command yet.
- Must work on Windows.

Utility must provide changed paths from `git status --short`, staged vs unstaged if practical, diff name-only, diff stat if practical, safe behavior when repo is not a git repo, and clear error or empty result according to CLI conventions.

Acceptance:
- Tests use temporary git repos.
- Handles clean repo, modified source file, modified test file, deleted file, and untracked file.
- Does not run destructive git commands.

tasks.title: Implement guard rule for generated/runtime edits
tasks.description:
Purpose: prevent agents from editing generated/runtime files manually.

Prerequisite:
Git diff collection utility exists.

Scope:
- Start guard rule engine internals.
- Add tests for generated/runtime violations.
- CLI may remain internal if full guard command is not ready.

Rule:
If changed files include `.vibecode/current/*`, `.vibecode/generated/*`, `.vibecode/logs/*`, `.vibecode/runs/*`, `.vibecode/tmp/*`, `.vibecode/cache/*`, or `.vibecode/index/*.generated.*`, guard reports a hard failure unless the task explicitly says generator behavior is being tested and the file is still ignored/untracked.

Acceptance:
- Rule returns failing result in tests for generated/runtime changes.
- Error explains: regenerate generated files; do not manually edit them.
- Human-maintained `.vibecode/architecture/*.md` is not treated as generated.

tasks.title: Implement guard rule for protected paths
tasks.description:
Purpose: enforce `.vibecode/checks/protected_paths.yaml`.

Scope:
- Build on protected path loader and git diff utility.
- Add guard rule internals and tests.
- Do not implement all guard rules yet if too broad.

Behavior:
- Changed protected paths require explicit task scope.
- If policy has `required_tests`, guard reports those tests as required.
- Generated paths are hard failures when manually edited.
- Human-maintained truth docs require explanation/handoff.

Acceptance:
- Guard detects changes under `.vibecode/architecture/`.
- Guard detects changes to core files such as `vibecode/context/scoring.py` and reports required tests.
- Output contains path, rule, severity, and recommended fix.

tasks.title: Implement README generated-block guard
tasks.description:
Purpose: protect README from uncontrolled agent rewrites.

Scope:
- Add guard logic for README changes.
- Define allowed generated block markers if they exist, or document that README is manual-only until generated blocks are introduced.
- Do not add README generated block automation in this task.

Behavior:
- If README changes outside allowed markers, guard should warn or fail depending on policy.
- If no markers exist, README changes are allowed only when task explicitly mentions README/docs.
- Do not block docs tasks.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover README changed during docs task and non-docs task.
- Error message is clear.
- No false failure for `docs/QUICKSTART.md` changes.

tasks.title: Implement architecture-change handoff guard
tasks.description:
Purpose: require handoff/history acknowledgement when architecture truth changes.

Scope:
- Guard rule internals and tests.

Behavior:
If changed files include `.vibecode/architecture/*.md`, guard requires at least one of `.vibecode/handoff/NOW.md` changed with a relevant note, `.vibecode/history/*.md` changed with a relevant summary, or an explicit override flag in future CLI.

Acceptance:
- Architecture doc changes without handoff/history fail guard.
- Architecture doc changes with handoff/history pass.
- Message explains that architecture truth changed and must be recorded.

tasks.title: Implement source/test mismatch guard warnings
tasks.description:
Purpose: warn when source changes occur without corresponding test changes, or tests change without source changes.

Scope:
- Guard rule internals.
- This rule should usually warn, not hard fail.

Behavior:
- Source changed without tests: warning with suggested tests.
- Tests changed without source: warning unless task is explicitly test-only.
- Use existing test_map/source pairing where possible.
- Do not require tests for docs-only changes.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover source-only, test-only, source+test, and docs-only.
- Warnings are clear and not overbearing.

tasks.title: Add guard CLI command
tasks.description:
Purpose: expose guard rules through `python -m vibecode.cli guard <repo>` and `vibecode guard <repo>`.

Prerequisite:
Initial guard rules exist.

Scope:
- Update CLI parser and dispatch.
- Add tests in CLI test module or guard-specific tests.
- Do not implement check runner or OpenCode run in this task.

Required behavior:
- `guard <repo>` inspects current git diff.
- Returns non-zero exit code for hard failures.
- Prints clear reasons.
- Prints warnings separately.
- Supports clean repo with zero findings.
- Uses protected paths policy if present.
- Handles missing `.vibecode/project.yaml` with clear error.

Acceptance:
- Hard violation returns non-zero.
- Warning-only returns zero unless a strict flag is introduced.
- CLI help includes guard.
- Tests pass.

tasks.title: Add guard report JSON output
tasks.description:
Purpose: store guard results as ignored runtime output for future run workflows.

Scope:
- Add optional or default write to `.vibecode/current/guard_result.json`.
- Ensure file is ignored.
- Do not make guard depend on previous guard result.

JSON should include timestamp, repo root, changed files, hard failures, warnings, policy files loaded, and required tests suggested.

Acceptance:
- Guard writes valid JSON report.
- Report is ignored/untracked.
- Tests validate JSON shape.

tasks.title: Implement required checks schema validation
tasks.description:
Purpose: make `.vibecode/checks/required_checks.yaml` enforce concrete commands before adding a runner.

Scope:
- Work in config/check loading code.
- Add tests.

Rules:
- Each check must have a name and command.
- `required` defaults to true unless explicitly false.
- Generic commands such as `tests`, `lint`, or `run checks` without a real command should be rejected or warned.
- Duplicate commands should be deduplicated or reported clearly.
- Purpose/description is optional but recommended.

Acceptance:
- Existing required checks load successfully.
- Invalid check files fail with clear error.
- Context pack required checks remain deduplicated.

tasks.title: Implement required checks runner
tasks.description:
Purpose: add `vibecode check <repo>` to run concrete commands from `.vibecode/checks/required_checks.yaml`.

Scope:
- Add check runner module and CLI command.
- Do not integrate with OpenCode run yet.
- Use subprocess safely.
- Must work on Windows.

Behavior:
- Load required checks.
- Run each command in repo root.
- Capture exit code, stdout/stderr snippets, and duration.
- Required failed check makes command exit non-zero.
- Optional failed check reports warning.
- Store result in `.vibecode/current/check_results.json`.

Acceptance:
- `python -m vibecode.cli check .` works.
- JSON report is valid and ignored/untracked.
- Tests use safe dummy commands.

tasks.title: Add check command docs and context integration
tasks.description:
Purpose: make required checks discoverable to agents and humans.

Scope:
- Update README/QUICKSTART minimally.
- Update context pack if needed to mention `vibecode check <repo>`.
- Do not add broad roadmap.

Acceptance:
- Docs say checks are defined in `.vibecode/checks/required_checks.yaml`.
- Docs say results are written to `.vibecode/current/check_results.json`.
- Context pack still lists exact check commands.
- Tests pass.

tasks.title: Implement handoff placeholder detection
tasks.description:
Purpose: detect empty/useless handoff files.

Scope:
- Add internal handoff validation utility.
- Do not implement full CLI command yet unless small.
- Tests required.

Rules:
- `NOW.md` must contain concrete current state, not only heading.
- `NEXT.md` must contain concrete next steps, not only heading.
- `BLOCKERS.md` must say either no hard blocker or list blockers.
- Placeholder phrases such as `TODO`, `TBD`, `placeholder`, empty bullets, or only headings should fail.

Acceptance:
- Real current handoff passes.
- Placeholder handoff fails with clear message.
- Tests cover empty, heading-only, placeholder text, and valid content.

tasks.title: Implement handoff architecture-change validation
tasks.description:
Purpose: ensure architecture truth changes are reflected in handoff or history.

Scope:
- Extend handoff validation utility with git diff input.
- Tests.

Behavior:
- If `.vibecode/architecture/*.md` changed, validation requires handoff file changed with relevant mention, or history summary added, or explicit override in future CLI.
- If only source/test changed, architecture handoff requirement does not apply.

Acceptance:
- Architecture change without handoff fails.
- Architecture change with handoff passes.
- Message is clear and actionable.

tasks.title: Add handoff-check CLI command
tasks.description:
Purpose: expose handoff validation as `vibecode handoff-check <repo>`.

Scope:
- Update CLI parser/dispatch.
- Add tests.

Behavior:
- Checks NOW/NEXT/BLOCKERS.
- Checks architecture-change handoff requirement using current git diff.
- Returns non-zero when handoff is invalid.
- Writes optional report to `.vibecode/current/handoff_check.json` if useful.

Acceptance:
- CLI help includes handoff-check.
- Placeholder handoff fails.
- Valid handoff passes.
- Architecture doc change without handoff fails.

tasks.title: Define history summary policy
tasks.description:
Purpose: make `.vibecode/history/` useful as compressed project memory, not noisy logs.

Scope:
- Update `.vibecode/history/README.md`.
- Add docs if needed.
- No automation yet.

Policy:
- `.vibecode/history/README.md` is committed.
- `.vibecode/history/*.md` may be committed only when it contains durable project truth.
- `.vibecode/runs/*` is always runtime output and ignored.
- History summaries are not raw logs.
- A history summary contains Task, Changed files, Behavior changed, Tests run, Decisions, and Follow-up.

Acceptance:
- Policy is explicit and short.
- AGENTS/context/handoff docs do not conflict with it.

tasks.title: Implement history summary template writer
tasks.description:
Purpose: provide a safe way to create durable history summaries without dumping raw logs.

Scope:
- Add internal utility and optional CLI command such as `vibecode history new <repo> --task "<task>"`.
- If CLI scope is too large, implement utility plus tests only.
- Do not integrate with OpenCode run yet.

Behavior:
- Creates `.vibecode/history/YYYYMMDD-HHMM-task-summary.md`.
- Filename is sanitized.
- File contains headings: Task, Changed files, Behavior changed, Tests run, Decisions, Follow-up.
- Does not automatically create a summary for every trivial command.

Acceptance:
- Tests validate filename format and template content.
- Does not overwrite existing summaries.
- History summaries are human-maintained and may be committed when meaningful.

tasks.title: Integrate handoff/history requirements into context pack
tasks.description:
Purpose: ensure agents know when and how to update handoff/history.

Scope:
- Update context renderer and tests.

Context pack should say:
- Handoff files are required for meaningful changes.
- Architecture truth changes require handoff or history.
- History summaries are durable memory, not run logs.
- `.vibecode/runs/*` remains ignored runtime state.

Acceptance:
- Context pack includes concise handoff/history rules.
- It does not dump all history content.
- Tests cover rendering.

tasks.title: Add OpenCode availability detection
tasks.description:
Purpose: prepare for run adapter by detecting whether OpenCode is installed without launching a coding session.

Scope:
- Add small adapter module, for example `vibecode/adapters/opencode.py`.
- Add tests with fake PATH or monkeypatch.

Behavior:
- Detect configured OpenCode command or default `opencode`.
- Provide clear status: available, missing, command failed.
- Do not start an agent session.

Acceptance:
- Missing OpenCode produces actionable error.
- Tests do not require real OpenCode installed.

tasks.title: Add OpenCode permission profile templates
tasks.description:
Purpose: create safe/fast/audit permission profiles for future OpenCode runs.

Scope:
- Add `.vibecode/agents/` policy docs/templates or generated-on-init files.
- Decide whether templates are human-maintained and committed.
- Add tests for init preserving custom profiles.

Profiles:
- safe: read allow, grep/glob allow, edit ask, bash ask, generated files deny
- fast: edit allow, bash ask, guard after run
- audit: read only, no edit, no write

Acceptance:
- Profiles exist in clear files like `.vibecode/agents/opencode.safe.json`.
- `init` creates missing templates but does not overwrite human-edited profiles.
- Docs warn that sensitive projects should use audit/safe.

tasks.title: Build OpenCode prompt/run plan assembly
tasks.description:
Purpose: assemble everything needed for an OpenCode run without executing OpenCode yet.

Scope:
- Add run planning utility.
- Do not launch OpenCode in this task.
- Tests required.

Run plan should include repo root, task, dirty git status, index freshness status, context pack path, opencode prompt path, permission profile, preflight warnings, and commands that would be run.

Acceptance:
- Either `vibecode run-plan <repo> --platform opencode --task "..."` exists, or an internal utility with tests exists if CLI is too soon.
- Dirty repo warning exists.
- Missing/stale index warning exists.
- No agent process is started.

tasks.title: Implement OpenCode run preflight
tasks.description:
Purpose: prevent running agents into a dirty/stale state without warning.

Scope:
- OpenCode run adapter preflight only.
- Do not launch OpenCode unless explicitly part of this task after preflight is tested.

Preflight checks:
- repo is git repo
- `.vibecode/project.yaml` exists
- generated index exists and is fresh or can be regenerated
- current git status is clean or user explicitly allows dirty state
- required permission profile exists
- OpenCode command is available
- generated/runtime files are ignored

Acceptance:
- Dirty repo blocks or warns according to flag.
- Stale/missing index is detected.
- Preflight has tests using fake repos.
- No real OpenCode required in tests.

tasks.title: Implement OpenCode run adapter with fake runner tests
tasks.description:
Purpose: add `vibecode run <repo> --platform opencode --task "..."` using a fake OpenCode binary in tests.

Prerequisites:
OpenCode detection and preflight exist.

Flow:
1. check git status
2. generate or refresh index if missing/stale
3. generate context pack
4. generate opencode prompt
5. invoke configured OpenCode command with safe prompt input
6. capture stdout/stderr/exit code
7. write session metadata under `.vibecode/runs/`
8. do not commit generated/runtime files

Acceptance:
- Tests use fake OpenCode script/command and do not require real OpenCode.
- Run metadata is stored under ignored `.vibecode/runs/`.
- Failed OpenCode command reports clear error.
- Dirty repo behavior is safe.

tasks.title: Run post-OpenCode guard and check pipeline
tasks.description:
Purpose: make `vibecode run` useful as a controlled agent workflow, not just a launcher.

Prerequisites:
- guard command exists
- check command exists
- handoff-check exists
- OpenCode run adapter exists

Post-run flow:
1. collect git diff summary
2. run `vibecode guard`
3. run `vibecode check`
4. run `vibecode handoff-check`
5. write run summary to `.vibecode/runs/<timestamp>/summary.json`
6. print concise result

Acceptance:
- Hard guard failure causes run to be marked failed.
- Required check failure causes run to be marked failed.
- Missing handoff causes run to be marked incomplete.
- Tests use fake OpenCode and fake checks.

tasks.title: Add OpenCode run diff summary
tasks.description:
Purpose: show user what changed after an agent run.

Scope:
- Add diff summary utility or reuse git diff collection.
- Integrate into run output.

Summary should include changed files, added/deleted files, source/test/docs/generated categories, protected path touches, whether generated/runtime files changed, and suggested next action.

Acceptance:
- Summary is human-readable.
- JSON summary is stored in run metadata.
- Tests cover representative diffs.

tasks.title: Document OpenCode run workflow
tasks.description:
Purpose: document the safe OpenCode workflow once implementation exists.

Scope:
- Update README and QUICKSTART minimally.
- No broad marketing copy.

Docs should show context-only generation, run plan/preflight, audit/safe/fast permission profiles, running fake or real OpenCode, post-run guard/check/handoff behavior, where run metadata is stored, and that generated/runtime files remain ignored.

Acceptance:
- Docs match actual commands.
- No claim that VibecodeApp edits code itself.
- No GUI/MCP claims.

tasks.title: Keep canonical CLI command set small and consistent
tasks.description:
Purpose: prevent CLI bloat and command-name drift.

Scope:
- Audit CLI parser/help/docs/tests.
- Keep canonical commands: `init`, `index`, `map`, `context`, `validate`, `export-agents`, `guard`, `check`, `handoff-check`, `run`, and `project` once registry exists.
- Do not add aliases unless documented.

Acceptance:
- README, QUICKSTART, CLI help, and tests use consistent command names.
- No stale command names like `generate-context` unless supported as documented alias.

tasks.title: Implement project registry storage
tasks.description:
Purpose: allow selecting repos by project name instead of passing paths every time.

Scope:
- Add registry module.
- No GUI.
- Use local user-level file, likely `~/.vibecode/projects.yaml`.
- Tests must not write to real home; use temp HOME/app data override.

Registry fields:
- project name
- repo path
- project id if known
- last used timestamp if useful
- active project name

Acceptance:
- Can read/write registry.
- Handles Windows paths.
- Handles missing/deleted repo paths gracefully.
- Registry file is outside project repo and not committed.

tasks.title: Add project registry CLI commands
tasks.description:
Purpose: expose project registry via CLI.

Commands:
- `vibecode project add <name> <path>`
- `vibecode project use <name>`
- `vibecode project list`
- `vibecode project remove <name>`
- `vibecode project current`

Scope:
- Update CLI parser and tests.
- Do not integrate all existing commands with active project yet.

Acceptance:
- Commands work with temp registry in tests.
- Duplicate names require explicit overwrite or fail clearly.
- Removing active project clears or updates active selection safely.

tasks.title: Allow commands to use active project when repo is omitted
tasks.description:
Purpose: improve CLI UX after registry exists.

Commands that should support active project fallback:
- `index`
- `map`
- `context`
- `validate`
- `guard`
- `check`
- `handoff-check`
- `run`

Behavior:
- If repo argument is provided, use it.
- If omitted, use active project from registry.
- If no active project, error clearly.
- Do not guess from current directory unless command semantics already support `.`.

Acceptance:
- `vibecode project use STOCKS` then `vibecode context --task "..."` works.
- Error tells user to pass repo or run `vibecode project use`.
- Tests cover Windows-style paths.

tasks.title: Update docs for project registry workflow
tasks.description:
Purpose: document the ergonomic workflow without replacing explicit-path commands.

Docs should show:
- explicit path workflow still works
- registry workflow with `vibecode project add STOCKS C:\DATA\PROJECTS\STOCKS`, `vibecode project use STOCKS`, and `vibecode context --task "..."`
- registry lives outside repo
- registry is local machine state, not committed

Acceptance:
- README/QUICKSTART are concise.
- Docs do not imply GUI exists.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end smoke for controlled agent preparation
tasks.description:
Purpose: verify the full pre-agent workflow without launching a real agent.

Flow in temp repo:
1. `vibecode init`
2. `vibecode index`
3. `vibecode context --task "..."`
4. `vibecode export-agents`
5. `vibecode guard`
6. `vibecode check` with safe dummy check
7. `vibecode handoff-check`

Acceptance:
- Test uses temp repo and safe commands.
- Generated/runtime files stay ignored/untracked.
- Context pack includes relevant files.
- AGENTS export is safe.
- Guard/check/handoff commands produce expected statuses.

tasks.title: Add stale index detection
tasks.description:
Purpose: warn when generated index was built for an older commit or file state.

Behavior:
- Compare last index git commit/path set to current HEAD/status where available.
- If repo changed since index, warn or require re-index before `context`, `guard`, or `run`.
- Do not block docs-only changes unless policy says so.
- Provide clear command: `vibecode index <repo>`.

Acceptance:
- Stale index warning appears in context/run preflight.
- Fresh index reports clean.
- Tests cover changed file after index.

tasks.title: Add context-pack quality regression test
tasks.description:
Purpose: ensure future context packs remain useful and compact.

Test using VibecodeApp repo or fixture:
- generate context for `Improve relevant-file scoring`
- assert relevant files include scoring.py and relevant tests
- assert generated/runtime paths are absent from top relevant files
- assert required checks are deduplicated
- assert protected path section exists
- assert handoff section exists
- assert context length is within limit

Acceptance:
- Test catches scoring/context regressions.
- Test does not require exact full markdown.

tasks.title: Final control-layer audit report
tasks.description:
Purpose: after implementing AGENTS, guard, check, handoff, history, OpenCode run adapter, and project registry, produce a factual status report.

Scope:
- Read-only audit.
- Do not implement features.
- Do not regenerate generated artifacts unless explicitly needed for validation.

Report must answer:
- What is implemented?
- What commands exist?
- Which files are source of truth?
- Which files are generated/runtime?
- Are generated files ignored?
- Does guard catch protected/generated changes?
- Does check runner execute real commands?
- Does handoff-check prevent empty handoffs?
- Does OpenCode run adapter require clean/stale-aware preflight?
- Does project registry work?
- What remains before GUI?

Acceptance:
- Report contains no false claims about GUI/MCP/swarm.
- Tests pass.
- Report distinguishes implemented, partial, and not started.
